summary: Audit every Thronefall game system vs the MP mod sync coverage to find unsynced state that diverges between peers

coverage.summary: Gold is host-authoritative and well covered for the main flows: ResourceSync reliably pushes GlobalData.Internal.Balance/NetWorth host->clients on change; clients predict spending via GlobalData.LocalBalanceDelta which is reconciled by ConfirmBuild/CancelBuild packets (PacketHandler.cs:376-405) and host-side cost validation (HandleBuildOrUpgrade). PlayerInteractionPatch reroutes AddCoin (server-only credit) and SpendCoins (shared balance); TreasuryUIPatch and BuildingInteractorPatch.InteractionHold mirror the shared balance into the local player's private 'balance' field for vanilla UI/affordability; CostDisplayPatch.CancelFill/Deny correctly credit back local prediction on cancel/deny (so the refund-coin path is NOT a gap). Enemy coin drops are synced via EnemySpawnPacket.Coins (host-computed Spawn.goldCoinsPerEnemy, masking its Random.Range), dawn harvest is deterministically reassigned to the closest player, and Royal Mint / Treasure Hunter bonuses are routed through server-side GlobalData.Balance. Physical Coin objects are local-only on every peer by design, which is safe for balance but leaks in the specific cases below.

HpSync periodically (3s force + on-change) replicates Hp, maxHp and KnockedOut for Player/Building/Ally/Enemy identifiers and applies corrections via Revive/Heal/TakeDamage with HpPatch.AllowHealthChangeOnClient. HpPatch (On.Hp.TakeDamage/Heal/ScaleHp/SetHpToMaxHp) blocks client-side mutation of any identified target, making damage/heal host-authoritative; DamageFeedbackPacket replays OnReceiveDamage on clients; ManualAttackPacket replays player actives (incl. PlayerShieldMA invulnerability) on all peers; UnitRespawnerForBuildings is host-only; dawn revive (ReviveAllKnockedOutPlayerUnitsAndBuildings) and dusk SetAllUnitsWithTagInvulnerable run deterministically on every peer from synced DayNight packets. AutoAttack target selection is divergent per peer (UnityEngine.Random in TargetPriority.FindRandomObjectInRange and AutoAttackLowestInRangeMulti.Update, plus cooldown randomization) but is cosmetic in itself because damage is gated and positions/hp are corrected; ManualAttack/StabMA Random.Range calls are audio pitch only.

Commanding is host-authoritative and well-plumbed for the OLD game's feature set: the mod fully replaces CommandUnits.Update (C:\Users\nalar\tfmp_src\Main\Patches\CommandUnitsPatch.cs), gathers units server-side and broadcasts CommandAddPacket, resolves placement (including Random.value jitter) host-side and ships explicit HomePositions in CommandPlacePacket, and ships hold orders in CommandHoldPositionPacket; the "Command Units" button reaches the host via PlayerNetworkData.Shared/InputSync. Ally movement state (path+index, HomePosition, followingPlayer/seek target, walking-home, hasReachedHome, slowed) syncs at 10 Hz via AllyPathfinderSync, positions via PositionSync (Ally deviance correction), and HP/maxHp/KnockedOut plus revive via HpSync with client mutation gates in HpPatch. Unit respawn is host-gated (UnitRespawnerForBuildingsPatch) and propagates correctly through HpSync's Revive+SnapToNavmesh path; client pathfinding/leashing is suppressed (PathfindMovementPlayerunitPatch); mid-level upgrades (assassins training, commander, blacksmith, UpgradeUnitsOnEnable) re-run deterministically on every peer via ConfirmBuildPacket → BuildSlotPatch.HandleUpgrade.

Day/night transitions are host-authoritative and synced: night via NightCallPatch sending DayNightPacket(Night) and day via the replaced DayNightCycle.Update on the server, which broadcasts DayNightPacket(Day, NightLength) and a VictoryPacket when EnemySpawner.MatchOver (C:\Users\nalar\tfmp_src\Main\Patches\DayNightCyclePatch.cs, NightCallPatch.cs, PacketHandler.HandleDayNight sets currentNightLength from the packet before running SwitchToDayCoroutine, so score-relevant night length is exact on clients). Enemy spawning is host-driven via EnemySpawnPacket (wave/spawn index, position, id, coins) with wavenumber kept in lockstep because both peers run EnemySpawner.StartSpawning at dusk; treasure-hunter and royal-mint start gold are routed through the synced GlobalData.Balance (EnemySpawnerPatch). Resign, victory and restart are event-synced (ResignPacket/VictoryPacket/RestartLevelPacket; both set LevelData.beaten and run the end screen on every peer, so each client records its own win/unlocks). Pausing cannot freeze or speed up time in MP: UIFrameManagerPatch.ProcessFrameChange forces frame.freezeTime=false, and the vanilla "Change Timescale" toggle (PlayerMovement.ToggleTimeScale, gameplayTimeScale 1↔2) is unreachable because the mod's PlayerMovement.Update replacement never reads that input. Score and persistence are deliberately per-peer (each peer runs ScoreManager and writes its own LevelData) — mostly fine, but several inputs to that computation and the entire mid-run save system are NOT synced (gaps below).

Player input is owner-sampled into PlayerNetworkData.Shared (Move Horizontal/Vertical, Sprint, Sprint Toggle, Interact, Call Night, Command Units) and broadcast via SyncPlayerInputPacket/InputSync; movement is then re-simulated on every peer by PlayerMovementPatch with owner-authoritative positions corrected by PositionSync. Manual/active weapon attacks are triggered cross-peer via ManualAttackPacket (TryToAttack on all peers, damage host-authoritative through HpPatch), weapons are per-player via the WeaponRequest/Response handshake + WeaponEquipperPatch, and PlayerHpRegen is host-authoritative (Hp.Heal suppressed on clients, HpSync corrects). Per-player perk loadouts do not exist: the level-requester's perks are equipped on the host (LevelDataSync.HandleRequestPacket) and broadcast in SyncLevelDataPacket so every peer caches identical heavyArmor/racingHorse flags in PlayerMovement.Start for every pawn — consistent, no divergence. Scepter animation (PlayerSceptPatch), night-call charging (NightCallPatch + DayNightPacket), interaction focus (local-only by design, purchases host-validated), and the vanilla 2x-timescale toggle ("Change Timescale" — deliberately dead in the patched Update, which is the safe choice) are all accounted for. Remaining gaps below are in the slow debuff, target locking, the mouse-movement input channel, and two minor input-packet omissions.

The mod covers the main world-state surfaces well for CLASSIC mode: all EnemySpawner spawn-line randomness is host-rolled and replicated by index+position+coins via EnemySpawnPacket (EnemySpawnerPatch.UpdateSpawn runs only on the server; Spawn.GetRandomPointOnSpawnLine is never rolled on clients); per-enemy gold randomness (Spawn.Reset's Random.Range into goldCoinsPerEnemy) is neutralized by the packet's Coins field; CommandUnits' random formation offsets run server-only and ship explicit Home positions (CommandPlacePacket); enemy/ally pathfinding is host-computed (PathFinderMovementEnemyPatch suppresses client path completion) and synced; gold is host-authoritative (PlayerInteraction.AddCoin gated to server, ResourceSync); coins, audio-pitch rolls, AutoAttack/TargetPriority random target picks, Ghostqueen fire-bolt visuals and PathMesher wobble are cosmetic-only on clients because HpPatch blocks client-side damage to identified objects; GateOpener state is derived purely from synced player/unit positions (mod feeds it the synced lists); TelePlayer teleports route through PlayerMovement.TeleportTo → TeleportPlayerPacket; building choice randomness (BuildSlot.ExecuteUpgrade Random.Range over upgradeBranches) is captured because the chosen branch index is what gets broadcast (BuildSlotPatch.OnUpgradeChoiceComplete). The serious holes are in everything that bypasses the EnemySpawner pipeline (boss-summoned waves, death-spawned enemies), in generated game modes (eternal trials / endless seeds, game mode itself never synced), in per-peer singleplayer persistence (mid-match save resume), and in two patch-induced vanilla deviations (dropped spawn modifiers; double OnDusk).

confirmedGaps.what: Ghostqueen and KeyframedBoss spawn their minion waves locally on EVERY peer through vanilla Spawn.Reset/Spawn.Update (Ghostqueen/<SpawnGhosts>d__31 calls Spawn::Reset + Spawn::Update(Single); KeyframedBoss.Update calls Wave::Reset(true) and Wave.Update -> Spawn::Update). This bypasses EnemySpawnerPatch entirely, so the instantiated enemies never receive an Identifier on either host or client.
confirmedGaps.divergence: During boss fights each peer fights its own private set of minions: spawn positions use per-peer Random (Spawn.GetRandomPointOnSpawnLine -> Random.value), HpSync/PositionSync/EnemyPathfinderSync skip them (they iterate Identifier repositories only), and HpPatch's identifier==null bypass (HpPatch.cs:28-32) lets the client damage/kill its local copies independently. Host minions deal the real damage to allies/buildings; client sees ghosts attacking nothing or already-dead minions. PacketHandler.HandleDayNight's day-transition cleanup (PacketHandler.cs:163-168) kills only Identifier.GetGameObjects(Enemy), so leftover client-side minions survive into the day permanently.
confirmedGaps.fix: Hook On.Spawn.Update (HookGen emits it; Spawn.Update(Single) is a normal instance method). Because EnemySpawnerPatch already replaced the EnemySpawner.Update spawn loop (it never calls vanilla Spawn.Update), this hook fires only for the boss paths (Ghostqueen direct, KeyframedBoss via Wave.Update). Host: replicate EnemySpawnerPatch.UpdateSpawn — decrement waitBeforeNextSpawn, GetRandomPointOnSpawnLine with the same LargeUnit/Flying constraint selection, coins from goldCoinsPerEnemy[spawnedUnits], assign Identifier(IdentifierType.Enemy, _nextEnemyId++), and broadcast a new BossSpawnPacket via Plugin.Instance.Network.Send(packet, handleLocal:true). Client: no-op the orig call. CRITICAL DETAIL: the packet cannot address the Spawn via EnemySpawner.waves indices (boss Spawns are private fields Ghostqueen::waveToSpawn / KeyframedBoss::waveToSpawn, verified in IL); address them instead by a stable key, e.g. (bossComponentType, Object.FindObjectsOfType index or the boss's own Identifier id, spawnIndex-within-Wave). The client handler must, like EnemySpawnerPatch.SpawnEnemy, both instantiate the prefab with the synced Identifier AND advance the local Spawn's private spawnedUnits/finished fields (both exist in IL: Spawn::spawnedUnits, Spawn::finished) so the client's vanilla boss coroutines (Ghostqueen's 'while !Spawn.Finished' loop, KeyframedBoss's Wave.HasFinished/waveRunning logic) terminate in lockstep with the host. Optionally, as belt-and-braces, extend PacketHandler.HandleDayNight's cleanup to also kill TagManager-tagged EnemyOwned objects lacking an Identifier.
confirmedGaps.reasoning:
Re-derived independently; every cited fact verified against IL and mod source.

GAME SIDE (tf_api IL dumps): (1) Ghostqueen.Start calls Spawn::Reset(true) on its private 'Spawn Ghostqueen::waveToSpawn' field, and Ghostqueen/<SpawnGhosts>d__31.MoveNext loops { Spawn::Update(1f) ... } until Spawn::get_Finished() — exactly as claimed. (2) KeyframedBoss.Update calls Wave::Reset(true) on its private 'Wave KeyframedBoss::waveToSpawn', then Wave::Update() + Wave::HasFinished() while waveRunning; Wave.Update() iterates spawns calling Spawn::Update(difficultyMulti). (3) Vanilla Spawn.Update(Single) calls Spawn.GetRandomPointOnSpawnLine, which starts with 'call System.Single UnityEngine.Random::get_value()' (per-peer RNG, positions diverge), then 'call UnityEngine.Object::Instantiate<GameObject>(...)' (IL line 142) and increments Spawn::spawnedUnits / sets Spawn::finished. These boss Spawn/Wave objects are private fields of the boss components — NOT entries in EnemySpawner.instance.waves — so they are unreachable by the existing EnemySpawnPacket addressing (wave index + spawn index into EnemySpawner.waves).

MOD SIDE: (1) C:\Users\nalar\tfmp_src\Main\Patches\EnemySpawnerPatch.cs hooks only On.EnemySpawner.Start/Update/OnStartOfTheDay; the server-only spawn replacement (UpdateSpawn, lines 95-141) and Identifier assignment (SpawnEnemy, lines 157-186) fire only for EnemySpawner-driven waves. Grep across Main for On.Spawn / On.Wave / On.Ghostqueen / On.KeyframedBoss: zero matches; the only attribute-based Harmony patches are CommandUnitsShouldShowUnitTypesPatch and MinimapRendererPatch (irrelevant). So vanilla Spawn.Update runs unpatched on EVERY peer for boss waves, spawning unidentified local enemies at per-peer-random positions. (2) All syncs enumerate Identifier repositories only: HpSync.cs lines 20-47 and EnemyPathfinderSync.cs line 19 iterate Identifier.GetIdentifiers(...), so these minions are invisible to HpSync/PositionSync/EnemyPathfinderSync. (3) HpPatch.cs lines 28-32: targets with no Identifier bypass the client damage gate, so each client kills its private copies locally while the host's copies (the authoritative ones) keep damaging allies/buildings — that damage arrives on the client via HpSync 'from nowhere'. (4) PacketHandler.cs HandleDayNight lines 163-168 kill only Identifier.GetGameObjects(IdentifierType.Enemy) on day transition, so unidentified client-local minions persist into the day. (5) Additional aggravator the claim missed: PathFinderMovementEnemyPatch.cs suppresses OriginalOnPathComplete/BackupOnPathComplete on ALL clients with no Identifier check and FindMoveToTarget returns the never-synced 'seekToTargetPos' field — so the client-local boss minions are also movement-broken (frozen/teleport-target garbage), making the client-side boss fight visually and mechanically nonsense.

NOT already covered: the boss object itself likely gets an Identifier (if activated through an EnemySpawner wave via the spawnLine==enemyPrefab.transform branch of EnemySpawnerPatch.SpawnEnemy), so boss HP syncs — but the minion waves it spawns do not, and nothing else (force-updates, HpSync, day cleanup) corrects them.

SEVERITY: critical is justified. Boss fights are the finale of progression levels; the host's real minions damage the shared base while the client literally cannot see or fight them (its own copies are at different random positions, frozen, and killing them does nothing), and leftovers persist past the night. This materially affects win/loss on boss levels and core progression.

confirmedGaps.what: LocalGamestate.selectedGameMode/gameModeSet (static, set only on the peer that clicks the UI: LevelSelectUIFrameHelper.TransitionToSelectedLevel calls set_SelectedGameMode(Classic); the Eternal Trials UI sets EternalTrial locally) and the entire EternalTrialsRunManager run state (currentRun.stage, currentRun.currentStageSeed, per-peer save file at SavePath) are never synchronized. EnemySpawner.Start generates the waves list per peer from EternalTrialsRunManager.CurrentRun.currentStageSeed via EternalWaveGenerator.GenerateWaves when isInEternalTrialMode. The mod has zero GameMode/Eternal handling (grep for GameMode|Eternal|endless in tfmp_src returns only EnemySpawnerPatch.cs:78 InfinitelySpawning).
confirmedGaps.divergence: If the host enters an Eternal Trials stage, the scene transition is propagated (RequestLevelPacket/Transition) but each peer builds a DIFFERENT waves list: the host from its own run seed, the client from its own (different or nonexistent) run, or from the authored Classic list if its currentMode stays Classic. EnemySpawnPacket addresses enemies by waves[wave].spawns[index], so the client spawns entirely different enemy types/counts or throws IndexOutOfRange; victory (wavenumber >= waves.Count-1) and the WaitThenTriggerEndOfMatchScreen branch (EternalTrialsRunManager.OnVictory/OnDefeat/AddScore run per peer, per-peer mode) diverge completely. Additionally the replaced DayNightCycle.Update never calls original, so vanilla automatedDaytime/automatedNighttime countdowns (remainingAutoDayTime/remainingAutoNightTime → auto SwitchToNight/SwitchToDayCoroutine) are dead on all peers, which ET stages rely on.
confirmedGaps.fix: Minimal guard (recommended now): block ET in MP at both ends. In SceneTransitionManagerPatch.TransitionToScene (initiating peer, before building RequestLevelPacket) and authoritatively in LevelDataSync.HandleRequestPacket (host), refuse when LocalGamestate.GameModeSet && LocalGamestate.SelectedGameMode == LocalGamestate.GameMode.EternalTrial (verified static property) || System.Array.IndexOf(EternalTrialsRunManager.mapRotation, packet.To) >= 0 (mapRotation is a public static string[] — verified), show a dialog via the existing ThronefallMP.UI UIManager dialog pattern, and force LocalGamestate.SelectedGameMode = GameMode.Classic so a peer who opened the ET menu doesn't carry the static into the next MP level (EnemySpawner.Start consumes it cross-scene). Full fix (larger feature, later): add Mode(byte)+Stage(int)+Seed(int) to RequestLevelPacket/SyncLevelDataPacket filled by the host from EternalTrialsRunManager.CurrentRun; on clients in LevelDataSync.HandleSyncPacket before SceneTransitionManagerPatch.Transition, set LocalGamestate.SelectedGameMode and Traverse-write a synthetic EternalTrialsRun (fields stage, currentStageSeed, currentMap — verified in IL) into static EternalTrialsRunManager.currentRun WITHOUT SaveRun; patch EternalTrialsRunManager.SaveRun/OnVictory/OnDefeat/AddScore to no-op on clients (host stays authoritative, mirrors VictoryPacket pattern) so client-local ET save files are never touched; and fix EnemySpawnerPatch.Start to preserve the ref-written goldBalanceAtStart in ET instead of restoring the pre-original value. Drop the automatedDaytime sentence from the report (symmetric regression, no evidence ET uses it) or file it as a separate low-severity vanilla-behavior issue.
confirmedGaps.reasoning: Re-derived independently; the core claim holds on every pillar. (1) Mod has zero ET/GameMode handling: grep for GameMode|Eternal|endless across C:\Users\nalar\tfmp_src\Main returns nothing at all. (2) IL LocalGamestate.set_SelectedGameMode is a STATIC setter ('ldarg.0; stsfld LocalGamestate/GameMode LocalGamestate::selectedGameMode; ldc.i4.1; stsfld bool gameModeSet') — set only on the peer that clicks the UI, never transmitted. (3) IL EnemySpawner.Start: currentMode is overwritten from SelectedGameMode only if GameModeSet; when get_isInEternalTrialMode (currentMode==1) it builds waves via 'EternalWaveGenerator::GenerateWaves(spawnLines, levelInfo, CurrentRun.stage, ref goldBalanceAtStart, CurrentRun.currentStageSeed, -1)'. (4) EternalTrialsRunManager state is entirely static and per-peer: get_CurrentRun lazy-loads from the peer's own save file or CreateFreshRun (own freshRunSeed); OnVictory advances stage and rolls 'new System.Random().Next(int.MinValue,int.MaxValue)' into currentStageSeed then SaveRun — guaranteed different across peers. (5) The transition IS propagated without mode/seed: SceneTransitionManagerPatch.TransitionToScene hook converts any online transition into RequestLevelPacket{To,From,Perks}; SyncLevelDataPacket carries only Level/PlayerData/Perks (LevelDataSync.cs:33-58); ET's LoadNextMap calls TransitionFromNullToLevel which flows into the hooked TransitionToScene (proven by the mod's own Transition() disabling _transitionHookEnabled around TransitionFromNullToLevel). So clients follow the host into the ET scene with mismatched/absent mode and a different run. (6) Consequence is concrete: EnemySpawnerPatch.cs:145 resolves 'EnemySpawner.instance.waves[waveNumber].spawns[spawnIndex]' from the packet — divergent waves lists mean wrong enemy prefab/count or ArgumentOutOfRangeException in the client packet handler; host's EnemySpawner.Update drives spawn packets from ITS waves list. (7) End-of-match: WaitThenTriggerEndOfMatchScreen IL calls EternalTrialsRunManager::OnVictory/OnDefeat gated on the per-peer static selectedGameMode, mutating/saving each peer's own run file. Not covered anywhere: HpSync/PositionSync address existing Identifiers and cannot correct a wrong prefab; VictoryPacket only syncs the win transition, not ET run state. ONE CORRECTION: the day/night sub-claim is overstated. DayNightCyclePatch.Update indeed never calls original, so automatedDaytime/automatedNighttime countdowns are dead — but identically on ALL peers (host included), so it is a symmetric vanilla-feature regression, not a sync divergence; and I found no evidence ET uses it (EternalWaveGenerator.GenerateWaves IL contains no DayNightCycle/SetToAutoDayLength calls; no callers found in EnemySpawner/PerkManager). That sentence should be dropped or re-filed separately. Bonus corroboration: EnemySpawnerPatch.Start caches goldBalanceAtStart before original and restores after — in ET, GenerateWaves writes the computed start gold through the ref param, which the mod then clobbers, so even ET starting gold is broken. Severity: matches rubric 'critical' (affects who wins/loses and core progression — wrong/missing enemies or handler exceptions break the match; ET run stage/seed/score/save diverge completely and a client's own local ET save can be corrupted), with the caveat that it only manifests when a peer enters Eternal Trials while online; Classic co-op is unaffected.

confirmedGaps.what: The vanilla mid-run save/continue system is entirely local and the mod never gates it. LevelSelectUIFrameHelper.OnShow calls MatchSaveLoadHandler.TryLoadRun(scene) and offers Continue (SetOverrideSave(false)) / Start New (SetOverrideSave(true)); on level load LocalMatchSaveLoad.Awake → TryLoad restores every SaveLoadEntity (EnemySpawner.wavenumber, ScoreManager.currentScore, building states, balances) when IsLoadingPermitted (CurrentSave != null && !OverwriteCurrentSave). Only the peer that opened the level UI (the host) has CurrentSave populated; clients transition via SyncLevelDataPacket/SceneTransitionManagerPatch.Transition which never calls TryLoadRun, and RequestLevelPacket carries no continue/fresh flag.
confirmedGaps.divergence: Host presses 'Continue' on a level it previously left mid-run (very common if the host also plays singleplayer): the host resumes at e.g. wave 6 with an upgraded kingdom, restored currentScore and hadRestarts=true, while every client starts the same scene at day 1 with nothing built. None of the restored state flows through the mod's build/spawn packets, so peers play different matches from the first frame. Conversely a stale client-side CurrentSave from an earlier session can set the client's MatchSave.hadRestarts (ScoreManager.OnLoad does this whenever a load pass runs with WaveCount>=2), silently zeroing that client's no-retry victory bonus.
confirmedGaps.fix: Force a fresh, identical run on every peer while online. In SceneTransitionManagerPatch.Transition (C:\Users\nalar\tfmp_src\Main\Patches\SceneTransitionManagerPatch.cs) — the single choke point all online scene loads pass through on host and clients (LevelDataSync.RequestHandler, HandleSyncPacket, RestartLevelPacket all route here) — set MatchSaveLoadHandler.OverwriteCurrentSave = true (verified public static via ldsfld/stsfld IL) before SceneTransitionManager.instance.TransitionFromNullToLevel(to). IsLoadingPermitted then returns false on every peer, LocalMatchSaveLoad.TryLoad no-ops, and the first in-level InitializeCurrentSave starts a clean MatchSave (and resets the flag, so singleplayer is unaffected once offline). Two recommended companions, same pattern as existing On.* hooks: (a) an On.LocalMatchSaveLoad.Save (or On.MatchSaveLoadHandler.SaveRun) hook that early-returns when Plugin.Instance.Network.Online, so MP matches stop overwriting each peer's singleplayer mid-run save every dusk/dawn; (b) cosmetic: an On.LevelSelectUIFrameHelper.OnShow postfix that, when Online, forces the hasNoSaveParent branch so the misleading Continue button never appears.
confirmedGaps.reasoning:
Re-derived independently from IL and mod source; every element of the claim checks out.

GAME SIDE (IL via tf_api against Assembly-CSharp.dll):
(1) LevelSelectUIFrameHelper.OnShow: 'call System.Void MatchSaveLoadHandler::TryLoadRun(System.String)' with LevelInteractor.lastActiveLevelInfo.sceneName; if CurrentSave != null && !ignoreSaves && !runComplete it activates hasSaveParent (Continue/New buttons), else noSavePlayButton. SetOverrideSave is literally 'ldarg.1 / stsfld System.Boolean MatchSaveLoadHandler::OverwriteCurrentSave / ret'. All MatchSaveLoadHandler fields (currentMap, currentSave, OverwriteCurrentSave) are accessed via ldsfld/stsfld — static, per-process, per-peer local state (the fix's "public static" premise is verified).
(2) MatchSaveLoadHandler.get_IsLoadingPermitted: returns false if CurrentSave == null OR OverwriteCurrentSave, plus an Eternal-Trials seed check — exactly as claimed.
(3) LocalMatchSaveLoad.Awake: if !disableAutomaticSaveLoading → FindObjectsOfType<SaveLoadEntity>(true) + TryLoad. TryLoad gates only on IsLoadingPermitted then runs ExecuteBeforeMainLoadPass/ExecuteLoad/ExecuteAfterMainLoadPass over every SaveLoadEntity — real gameplay state restoration.
(4) EnemySpawner: fields wavenumber/wavenumberLoadedFromSave/endlessSeedLoadedFromSave exist; Start applies wavenumberLoadedFromSave to wavenumber (IL lines 77-85). ScoreManager.OnLoad: TryLoadValue(guid,"currentScore", ref currentScore) and sets MatchSave::hadRestarts = true when the load pass runs with EnemySpawner.WaveCount >= 2 — confirming both the wave/score restore and the hadRestarts side effect (which zeroes the no-restart victory bonus).
(5) LocalMatchSaveLoad.Save (dusk/dawn callbacks) calls MatchSaveLoadHandler::InitializeCurrentSave + SaveRun — so MP sessions themselves autosave on EVERY peer each day/night transition, meaning every peer accumulates a local mid-run save for the level. This both makes the trigger condition common (next session the host is offered Continue) and corrupts peers' singleplayer continues with MP state.

MOD SIDE: Grep across C:\Users\nalar\tfmp_src\Main for MatchSaveLoad|TryLoadRun|OverwriteCurrentSave|CurrentSave|LocalMatchSaveLoad|SaveLoadEntity|hadRestarts|disableAutomaticSaveLoading returns ZERO matches; the Main\Patches\ directory has no save-related patch. C:\Users\nalar\tfmp_src\Main\Network\Packets\Game\RequestLevelPacket.cs carries only To/From/Perks — no continue/fresh flag. C:\Users\nalar\tfmp_src\Main\Network\Sync\LevelDataSync.cs (HandleRequestPacket → weapon handshake → SceneTransitionManagerPatch.Transition; HandleSyncPacket → Transition) and C:\Users\nalar\tfmp_src\Main\Patches\SceneTransitionManagerPatch.cs Transition/TransitionToScene never call TryLoadRun nor touch OverwriteCurrentSave. So the host's Continue/Start-New button press mutates only host-local statics, and each peer's LocalMatchSaveLoad.Awake then independently decides whether to restore a save from that peer's own disk/statics.

DIVERGENCE IS REAL AND UNCORRECTED: if the host presses Continue on a level with a mid-run save, the host's world resumes at wave N with restored score/buildings/balances while clients (CurrentSave null — they never ran TryLoadRun for that scene, or worse, restore their OWN unrelated stale save since CurrentSave/OverwriteCurrentSave are process-lifetime statics and a client browsing the level UI also calls TryLoadRun) start at day 1. None of the restored state replays through build/spawn/gold packets; host-authoritative wave-N spawn packets then hit a client world that is structurally different from frame one (mismatched buildings/upgrades also undermine Identifier/BuildSlot mapping for subsequent build packets). HpSync/PositionSync cannot correct entities and upgrade states that simply don't exist on the client. Not covered by any known/handled item (mutator fix, VictoryPacket, walls, revive panel are unrelated).

SEVERITY: critical — peers play different matches from the first frame; the trigger is common because MP itself writes mid-run saves on every peer each dusk/dawn; and it directly affects score/progression (restored currentScore, hadRestarts=true killing the no-restart victory bonus, SubmitScoreAndSave recording divergent results).

confirmedGaps.what: Both bosses spawn their adds through their own private Wave/Spawn objects, bypassing the mod's EnemySpawner pipeline entirely. KeyframedBoss.Update (Eismoloch; IL: ldfld Wave KeyframedBoss::waveToSpawn → Wave::Reset → Wave::Update → vanilla Spawn.Update) and Ghostqueen/<SpawnGhosts>d__31.MoveNext (Spawn::Reset → Spawn::Update(Single)) run unguarded on EVERY peer. Vanilla Spawn.Update calls Spawn.GetRandomPointOnSpawnLine (UnityEngine.Random.value) and Object.Instantiate locally, and nothing assigns an Identifier component to the spawned enemies.
confirmedGaps.divergence: On boss levels each peer instantiates its own independent set of adds at different random positions. The sync layer (PositionSync/HpSync/EnemyPathfinderSync all iterate Identifier.GetIdentifiers) never sees them. On clients PathFinderMovementEnemyPatch suppresses path completion, so client copies stand frozen at their spawn point forever; the host's copies are the real ones that walk and damage buildings (that damage syncs via HpSync, so clients see buildings hit 'by nothing'). The mod's dawn cleanup (PacketHandler.HandleDayNight kills only Identifier.GetGameObjects(IdentifierType.Enemy)) never removes the client ghosts, so they accumulate for the rest of the match.
confirmedGaps.fix:
Hook On.Spawn.Update (the Single-arg overload; the mod's own EnemySpawnerPatch never calls vanilla Spawn.Update, so this hook fires only for boss waves and any other direct Wave.Update callers — no interference with the existing EnemySpawner pipeline).

Server side: replicate the existing EnemySpawnerPatch.UpdateSpawn flow — run the timer bookkeeping (waitBeforeNextSpawn/interval), roll GetRandomPointOnSpawnLine with the 3-way constraint, allocate the next enemy id from the shared counter, and broadcast a new BossSpawnPacket { SpawnRef, Id, Position, Coins } via Plugin.Instance.Network.Send(packet, handleLocal: true). SpawnRef must identify the Spawn instance: build a small registry at level load (e.g., in SceneTransitionManagerPatch or a new BossPatch.Apply on scene load) that collects Object.FindObjectsOfType<KeyframedBoss>() / <Ghostqueen>() in deterministic order (sort by hierarchy path) and indexes their waveToSpawn Spawns — both peers load the same scene so indices match. Handle vanilla Spawn.Update's two non-Instantiate branches too (TaggedObject==null → SetActive+finished, and enemyPrefab.scene.isLoaded → SetActive): mirror the existing `spawnLine == enemyPrefab.transform` handling in EnemySpawnerPatch.SpawnEnemy.

Client side: suppress instantiation but still run the local timer/counter bookkeeping (decrement waitBeforeNextSpawn, increment spawnedUnits, set finished — all field names verified in IL: finished, waitBeforeNextSpawn, interval, spawnedUnits, count, goldCoinsPerEnemy) WITHOUT consuming UnityEngine.Random and WITHOUT Instantiate, so KeyframedBoss.Update's Wave.HasFinished()/waveRunning stage logic converges on both peers.

confirmedGaps.fix: In CommandUnitsPatch.AddUnit (C:\Users\nalar\tfmp_src\Main\Patches\CommandUnitsPatch.cs:299-337), mirror current vanilla OnUnitAdd by inserting, after the AUTO_Commanded tag add (line 325) and BEFORE the commander early-return branch (line 327) to match vanilla IL ordering: var rpEquipped = Traverse.Create(self).Field<bool>("royalProtectionEquipped").Value; if (rpEquipped) { unit.GetComponent<Hp>().ScaleHp(Traverse.Create(self).Field<float>("royalProtectionHealthMulti").Value); } Both field names verified in CommandUnits IL (Start stores into CommandUnits::royalProtectionEquipped and CommandUnits::royalProtectionHealthMulti). The existing Contains(unit) dedup guard at the top of AddUnit prevents double-scaling on duplicate adds, matching vanilla.
confirmedGaps.reasoning: Re-derived independently and every link in the chain checks out. (1) Vanilla CommandUnits.OnUnitAdd IL applies hp.ScaleHp(royalProtectionHealthMulti) when royalProtectionEquipped; its only callers are two call sites in vanilla CommandUnits.Update (IL lines ~286 and ~321), and CommandUnits.Awake only sets the static instance (no event subscription), so since CommandUnitsPatch.Update never calls original, vanilla OnUnitAdd never runs on any peer. (4) HpPatch.ScaleHp (HpPatch.cs:52-65) lets the call through on the server and blocks it on clients, so only the host's value shrinks. (5) Hp.ScaleHp IL multiplies both Hp::hp and Hp::maxHp — permanent, compounding. CommandUnits.Start IL confirms royalProtectionHealthMulti = 1f / PerkManager.royalProtectionDamageTakenMultiplyer, and royalProtectionEquipped is read from PerkManager (perks now equipped on host per the fixed-items list), so the divide fires on host. Net effect: with Royal Protection equipped, every command->place (or death-while-commanded) cycle multiplies the unit's hp+maxHp by royalProtectionDamageTakenMultiplyer (<1) with no compensating scale-up, so commanded units permanently melt for all peers. This is the 'vanilla path suppressed by a patch without replacement' divergence class. No existing sync/packet/patch covers it. Severity stays high (visibly wrong gameplay, perk becomes a curse, can cost a match) rather than critical because it only triggers with that one perk equipped and does not directly corrupt win/loss handling or progression.

(b) Client handler: if EnemySpawner.instance exists, is endless (Traverse "endlessMode"), and Traverse "endlessSeed" != packet.Seed: set endlessSeed = packet.Seed, then regenerate `self.waves = self.endlessModeWaveGeneratorScript.GenerateWaves(self.GetComponentsInChildren<EnemySpawnLine>(), LevelProgressManager.instance.GetLevelInfoFromCurrentSceneName(), 3, ref dummyGold, packet.Seed, -1)` (discard the ref gold — starting balance is already host-authoritative via GlobalData.Balance in EnemySpawnerPatch.Start), and re-apply the vanilla post-generation perk passes (EliteGod eliteEnemies interval marking and RatGod goldCoins multiplier, copied from the Start IL) so client waves byte-match the host's. confirmedGaps.reasoning:
Re-derived from IL and mod source; every element of the claim checks out.

(1) GAME SIDE — verified in IL of EnemySpawner.Start (endless branch): after `ldfld EnemySpawner::endlessMode` and a null-check on `endlessModeWaveGeneratorScript`, it runs `endlessSeedLoadedFromSave = MatchSaveLoadHandler.TryLoadValue<int>(guid, "endlessSeed", ref endlessSeed)`; then if `MatchSaveLoadHandler.OverwriteCurrentSave` is true (brtrue to the reseed block) OR the seed wasn't loaded, it executes exactly the quoted sequence: `call Time::get_unscaledTime / ldc.r4 1000000 / mul / call Mathf::RoundToInt / call Random::InitState / ldc.i4 -1000000 / ldc.i4 1000000 / call Random::Range / stfld EnemySpawner::endlessSeed`, then `waves = endlessModeWaveGeneratorScript.GenerateWaves(GetComponentsInChildren<EnemySpawnLine>(), levelInfo, 3, ref goldBalanceAtStart, endlessSeed, -1)`. EnemySpawner.Awake only sets the singleton; `endlessMode` has no runtime setter, so it is a serialized per-level field and the branch runs identically (but with different seeds) on both peers loading the same scene.

(2) MOD SIDE — grep of C:\Users\nalar\tfmp_src\Main: zero matches for "endless" and zero for MatchSave/OverwriteCurrentSave/SaveLoad. Nothing covers the seed or the waves list.

(3) DIVERGENCE IS REAL, NOT SELF-CORRECTING — PacketHandler.HandleEnemySpawn → EnemySpawnerPatch.SpawnEnemy(wave, spawnIndex, ...) resolves `EnemySpawner.instance.waves[waveNumber].spawns[spawnIndex]` on the CLIENT's locally generated list (C:\Users\nalar\tfmp_src\Main\Patches\EnemySpawnerPatch.cs:145). With divergent seeds the client gets the wrong Spawn entry → wrong enemyPrefab, wrong eliteEnemies/taunt flags, wrong per-spawn counts, or an ArgumentOutOfRangeException when the host's spawn list is longer. The client's patched EnemySpawner.Update also gates wave completion on `self.waves[self.Wavenumber].HasFinished()` (EnemySpawnerPatch.cs:73) using its own spawn counts, so wave-finished state diverges too; wave preview markers and difficulty UI read the local waves list. HpSync/PositionSync sync by Identifier id and cannot correct a wrong prefab. A sibling issue exists for Eternal Trials (currentStageSeed from per-peer EternalTrialsRunManager.CurrentRun, visible in the same Start IL) but that is a separate claim.

(4) FIX SKETCH FLAW FOUND — the proposed client pre-Start field injection cannot work: the IL shows `endlessSeedLoadedFromSave` is ASSIGNED from TryLoadValue's return inside Start (overwriting any pre-set value), and when `MatchSaveLoadHandler.OverwriteCurrentSave` is true (the normal fresh-co-op-match case) the reseed runs regardless of the flag. The fix must regenerate waves after original instead. Fields `endlessSeed` (Int32) and `endlessSeedLoadedFromSave` (Boolean) do both exist per the type dump, so Traverse access is valid — just not at the proposed hook point.

Severity: high, not critical — classic/campaign levels are unaffected; but every endless-mode co-op session desyncs from night 1 (wrong/missing enemies on clients, possible handler exception), which is visibly wrong gameplay and effectively breaks that mode.

confirmedGaps.what: LocalMatchSaveLoad is a registered IDaytimeSensitive, and the mod's DawnCallAfterSunrise replacement faithfully invokes OnDawn_AfterSunrise on all daytimeSensitiveObjects, so EVERY peer writes a full mid-run snapshot of its own local view to its own disk each dawn (TriggerDelayedSave → Save → SaveLoadEntity.ExecuteSave → MatchSaveLoadHandler.SaveRun → File.WriteAllText). Because of that, after the first dawn every peer has CurrentSave != null, and the mod's restart flow (RestartLevelPacket → Transition(scene, scene) → scene reload → LocalMatchSaveLoad.Awake → TryLoad) makes each peer restore from ITS OWN last-dawn snapshot rather than a single authoritative state.
confirmedGaps.divergence: (a) 'Try Again' after defeat resumes each peer from a locally-saved snapshot; any divergence that existed at that dawn (in-flight builds, client-predicted balance, HP not yet synced, the client's locally-killed leftover enemies) is baked into different starting states, with no packet correcting buildings restored by the save system (BuildSlot loads bypass ConfirmBuildPacket). A peer whose save write failed or who restarted before the first dawn starts truly fresh while others resume. (b) Side effect on singleplayer progression: an MP session overwrites the peer's local mid-run save for that map with co-op state (multiple player objects' data, shared-gold balances), which the player is later offered to 'Continue' in singleplayer.
confirmedGaps.fix: Disable the vanilla mid-run save/load entirely while Network.Online, matching existing patch patterns: add a LocalMatchSaveLoadPatch with On.LocalMatchSaveLoad.Save += (orig, self) => { if (!Plugin.Instance.Network.Online) orig(self); } (method exists: private void LocalMatchSaveLoad.Save()), and block restores by hooking On.LocalMatchSaveLoad.TryLoad the same way (private void TryLoad(), called from Awake) — or equivalently set the verified public static field MatchSaveLoadHandler.OverwriteCurrentSave = true inside SceneTransitionManagerPatch.Transition/TransitionToScene when Online so IsLoadingPermitted is false on every MP scene load. Blocking Save() also fixes part (b) (no co-op overwrites of persistentDataPath/<map>.json); blocking TryLoad also neutralizes the sibling continue gap on the initiating peer. Result: RestartLevelPacket deterministically resets the level fresh on all peers. If resume-from-last-dawn is wanted later, it requires a host-authoritative snapshot packet (host serializes its MatchSave JSON and broadcasts it before the restart transition), not per-peer files.
confirmedGaps.reasoning: Re-derived end-to-end. Game IL: LocalMatchSaveLoad.Start registers as IDaytimeSensitive; OnDawn_AfterSunrise -> TriggerDelayedSave (4 frames) -> Save(), which requires LocalGamestate.CurrentState==InMatch (value 1) and not past the final wave, then calls MatchSaveLoadHandler.InitializeCurrentSave() (creates currentSave if null and consumes OverwriteCurrentSave -> false), ExecuteSave() on every SaveLoadEntity (BroadcastMessage "OnSave"), and MatchSaveLoadHandler.SaveRun() -> File.WriteAllText(persistentDataPath/currentMap.json). Saved state confirmed: BuildSlot (level + appliedUpgrades), EnemySpawner (wavenumber + endlessSeed), Hp, PlayerInteraction (balance). LocalMatchSaveLoad.Awake -> TryLoad() gated only by IsLoadingPermitted = CurrentSave != null && !OverwriteCurrentSave. Mod side: DayNightCyclePatch.cs:60-93 invokes OnDawn_AfterSunrise on all daytimeSensitiveObjects, and clients reach dawn via PacketHandler.HandleDayNight -> SwitchToDayCoroutine, so EVERY peer saves at EVERY dawn. LevelDataSync.cs:272-277 (RestartLevelPacket) -> SceneTransitionManagerPatch.Transition -> TransitionFromNullToLevel -> scene reload -> Awake -> TryLoad: each peer restores its OWN snapshot. Grep for SaveLoad|MatchSave|CurrentSave|SaveRun|OverwriteCurrentSave across Main returns ZERO matches — the mod never touches the save system, so nothing covers this. Post-restore, ResourceSync corrects balance and HpSync corrects HP, but BuildSlot.level/appliedUpgrades and EnemySpawner.wavenumber have NO correcting sync (ConfirmBuildPacket is event-only), so any dawn-time divergence (in-flight builds, the documented client wavenumber lag — see the mod's own comment at DayNightCyclePatch.cs:49-53 — or the known walls desync) is permanently baked into different restart states; a divergent client wavenumber further desyncs EnemySpawnerPatch.Update wave-finish bookkeeping since spawn packets are keyed by the host's wave index. The claim's part (b) also verifies and is worse than stated: only the level-initiating peer gets MatchSaveLoadHandler.TryLoadRun(map) (called solely from LevelSelectUIFrameHelper.OnShow), so other peers' dawn writes go to a stale currentMap path — overwriting a DIFFERENT map's singleplayer save (or a junk .json) with co-op data, while the initiator's SP save for the MP map is overwritten with co-op state; and an initiator with a pre-existing SP save resumes from it on restart-before-first-dawn while everyone else starts fresh. Three claim details are wrong but do not change the verdict: (1) a failed disk write does NOT cause fresh-start asymmetry (TryLoad uses the in-memory CurrentSave, populated before SaveRun's try/catch); (2) leftover enemies are not baked in (enemies have no OnSave and HandleDayNight force-kills identified enemies at day); (3) restored per-peer balance is transient because ResourceSync re-corrects it. Severity high (not critical): visibly wrong gameplay after the common Try Again flow (divergent buildings/armies/wave counters) plus unconditional SP-progression pollution, but victory/defeat is now packet-driven and waves/balance/HP remain host-authoritative.

confirmedGaps.what: ScoreManager.get_VictoryGoldBonus = CeilToInt(PlayerInteraction.instance.TrueBalance * victoryGoldBonusMultiplyer), where TrueBalance = this.balance + TagManager.coins.Count + CoinSpawner.AllCoinsLeftToBeSpawned. PlayerInteraction.Awake does 'stsfld PlayerInteraction::instance', so in MP the singleton points at whichever player object was instantiated LAST by PlayerManager.InstantiatePlayer. The mod mirrors the shared gold onto only the LOCAL player's balance field (TreasuryUIPatch.Update: targetPlayer=local player, balance=GlobalData.Balance each frame); remote players' balance stays 0 because AddCoin/SpendCoins are patched to bypass it. The mod explicitly re-binds PlayerMovement.instance to the local player but never PlayerInteraction.instance.
confirmedGaps.divergence: On any peer where the last-instantiated player object is a remote player (e.g. the host, whose local player id 0 is created first), the victory screen's gold bonus — and therefore the final total written to LevelData.highscore by TransitionFromGameplayToEndScreen (highscore = score+goldBonus+mutatorBonus+noRetryBonus) and fed into meta-level XP — is computed from balance=0 instead of the shared treasury. Peers see and permanently record different victory totals for the same match.
confirmedGaps.fix: Gate the singleton at its source instead of (or in addition to) rebinding it once. In PlayerInteractionPatch.Apply add 'On.PlayerInteraction.Awake += Awake;' with: private static void Awake(On.PlayerInteraction.orig_Awake original, PlayerInteraction self) { var data = self.GetComponent<PlayerNetworkData>(); if (data == null || data.IsLocal) original(self); } — original is just 'stsfld instance', so this makes the local player's component own PlayerInteraction.instance regardless of instantiation order (PlayerNetworkData.id/Player are assigned in PlayerManager.InstantiatePlayer lines 150-152 before SetActive(true) at line 157, so IsLocal is valid during Awake; the vanilla scene player keeps original behavior, matching the existing PlayerMovementPatch.Awake pattern). Optionally also set PlayerInteraction.instance next to the PlayerMovement.instance fix in PlayerManager.InstantiatePlayer (line 161-168) as a belt-and-suspenders rebind ('instance' static field verified in IL). TreasuryUIPatch.Update:69 already mirrors GlobalData.Balance onto that component every frame, so VictoryGoldBonus then computes from the shared treasury identically on all peers; this also resolves the WaitThenTriggerEndOfMatchScreen entry in the GlobalData.cs:16-19 TODO (and incidentally fixes the TutorialManager/DebugCoinDisplay readers listed there, which read the same singleton).
confirmedGaps.reasoning: Verified end-to-end from IL and mod source. (1) IL ScoreManager.get_VictoryGoldBonus: ldsfld PlayerInteraction::instance -> get_TrueBalance -> CeilToInt(* victoryGoldBonusMultiplyer); TrueBalance = this.balance + TagManager.coins.Count + CoinSpawner.AllCoinsLeftToBeSpawned; PlayerInteraction.Awake is literally 'stsfld instance' with no guard, so the LAST activated player object owns the singleton. (2) The mod destroys the vanilla scene player (PlayerMovementPatch.Awake line 61) and activates each networked clone in PlayerManager.InstantiatePlayer (SetActive(true), line 157), each running the unpatched PlayerInteraction.Awake — on the host (players iterated in insertion order from SetPrefab, host first) and on any peer after a mid-session join (Network.cs:172/321/338), instance ends up on a REMOTE player's component. (3) PlayerInteractionPatch.AddCoin/SpendCoins never call original, so no PlayerInteraction.balance is ever written by gameplay; only TreasuryUIPatch.Update:69 mirrors GlobalData.Balance onto the LOCAL player's component — remote components stay at 0. (4) Consumption is per-peer: PacketHandler.HandleVictory (line 220) sets LocalGamestate AfterMatchVictory locally; IL of LocalGamestate/<WaitThenTriggerEndOfMatchScreen>d__25.MoveNext calls TransitionFromGameplayToEndScreen(CurrentScore, VictoryGoldBonus, VictoryMutatorBonus, VictoryNoRetryBonus); its classic-mode branch writes LevelData::highscore = sum and sets totalScoreFromLastMatch/totalScoreFromLastMatchIsNewPersonalRecord, persisted per-peer (LevelData.highscoreBest via SaveScoreAndStatsToBestIfBest). So the host typically computes the gold bonus from balance=0 while the client computes it from the shared treasury — divergent end-screen totals and permanently divergent saved highscores for the same match. NOT already covered: no mod code touches PlayerInteraction.instance or ScoreManager.VictoryGoldBonus; the LevelDataSync fix (Main/Network/Sync/LevelDataSync.cs:141-146) covers only VictoryMutatorBonus; and the mod's own TODO at Main/GlobalData.cs:16-19 explicitly lists 'Balance -> Player.balance in LocalGamestate.WaitThenTriggerEndOfMatchScreen' as unhandled — the authors acknowledge this exact gap. Severity high, not critical: victory/defeat itself is already broadcast (VictoryPacket) and LevelData.beaten is set in SetState independent of score, so win/lose and level-unlock progression are unaffected; what diverges is the visible gold bonus and the permanently recorded highscore/highscoreBest. One correction to the claim: its fix sketch (rebind instance only in InstantiatePlayer) is insufficient, because any remote player instantiated AFTER the local one re-runs the unpatched Awake and overwrites the singleton again (host's typical order; every peer on a mid-session join).

confirmedGaps.what: ManualAttack.preferredTarget (the manual-attack target lock) is mutated by the LOCAL Rewired action "Lock Target" inside ManualAttack.Tick, which PlayerAttackPatch.Update runs for EVERY player pawn on EVERY peer. ManualAttack.Start sets `input = ReInput.players.GetPlayer(0)` — the local keyboard/controller — so one machine's Lock Target press mutates preferredTarget on ALL pawns on that machine (including other players' pawns), and a player's lock choice never leaves their machine. No packet or Shared field carries it ("Lock Target" appears nowhere in mod source).
confirmedGaps.divergence: FindAttackTarget(true) returns preferredTarget when in range (IL verified), and ManualAttack.Attack uses it to pick the damage target; damage is host-authoritative (HpPatch.TakeDamage blocks clients). So: a client locks an enemy, their local sim shows the active ability hitting the locked enemy, but the host computes the real damage against the default closest-priority target — or worse, against whatever enemy the HOST last locked, since the host's own Lock Target press re-targets the client pawn's ManualAttack on the host. Wrong enemy takes the hit; visibly wrong combat for any weapon's active ability.
confirmedGaps.fix:
Two parts, consistent with existing mod patterns:

1. Kill cross-pawn contamination locally: for non-local pawns, stop the vanilla Lock Target read from mutating preferredTarget. Cleanest: hook On.ManualAttack.Tick; if the owning pawn's PlayerNetworkData.IsLocal is false, snapshot Traverse field "preferredTarget" (and "preferredTargetPreviouslySet") before orig and restore after — this also neutralizes the local auto-reacquire branch for remote pawns. (Alternative: swap the private "input" field to ReInput.players.GetSystemPlayer() on non-local pawns, but the snapshot/restore approach also suppresses re-acquire divergence.)

2. Broadcast the owner's lock state by change-detection rather than only on button press (this covers press-to-lock, press-to-unlock, AND the auto-reacquire when the locked target dies): in PlayerAttackPatch.Update after attack.Value.Tick() on the LOCAL pawn, compare preferredTarget to a cached last value; on change, resolve the TaggedObject's Identifier and send LockTargetPacket { Player = data.id, Target = IdentifierData (Invalid for unlock) } via Plugin.Instance.Network.Send(packet, false) (already applied locally by vanilla Tick). Add PacketId.LockTarget + Handlers entry in PacketHandler that resolves the player's ManualAttack via player.GetComponent<PlayerInteraction>().ManualAttack (same lookup as HandleManualAttack) and sets Traverse.Create(attack).Field<TaggedObject>("preferredTarget") (and preferredTargetPreviouslySet = target != null). Field names "preferredTarget", "preferredTargetPreviouslySet", and "input" all verified present in the ManualAttack type dump.
confirmedGaps.reasoning:
Re-derived independently; every link in the claimed chain checks out in IL and mod source.

(1) Local-input mutation confirmed: ManualAttack.Start IL ends with `call Rewired.ReInput/PlayerHelper::get_players(); ldc.i4.0; callvirt GetPlayer(0); stfld Rewired.Player ManualAttack::input` — every ManualAttack instance on every machine binds to local Rewired player 0. ManualAttack.Tick IL reads `ldfld input; ldstr "Lock Target"; callvirt GetButtonDown` and toggles `stfld TaggedObject ManualAttack::preferredTarget` (set from FindAttackTargetWithInfiniteRange(false) when null, cleared when already set); it also has an auto-reacquire branch gated on `preferredTargetPreviouslySet` that re-locks the closest target when the locked one dies. Both `preferredTarget` (protected TaggedObject) and `preferredTargetPreviouslySet` (private bool) exist in the type dump.

(2) Cross-pawn contamination confirmed: C:\Users\nalar\tfmp_src\Main\Patches\PlayerAttackPatch.cs:16-18 calls attack.Value.Tick() unconditionally for EVERY player pawn (only the UI update at lines 19-23 is IsLocal-gated). Remote pawns do get ManualAttack instances on each machine — C:\Users\nalar\tfmp_src\Main\Patches\WeaponEquipperPatch.cs:35 calls AssignManualAttack per-pawn using the handshaked weapon. So one local "Lock Target" press toggles preferredTarget on ALL pawns' ManualAttacks on that machine, each resolving its own closest target.

(3) Gameplay effect confirmed: FindAttackTarget(true) IL returns preferredTarget when set and within targetPriorities[0].range; ManualAttack.Attack IL calls FindAttackTarget(true), takes `TaggedObject::get_Hp()` and passes it to Weapon::Attack. Attack() executes on every peer (ManualAttackPacket sent with handleLocal=true at PlayerInteractionPatch.cs:75-76; PacketHandler.cs:294-305 HandleManualAttack only calls TryToAttack — no target data). Damage is host-authoritative: HpPatch.cs:44-47 returns false on clients for identified targets. So the host's preferredTarget (null, or whatever the HOST last locked — including on the client's pawn) decides the real damage target, while the locking client's screen shows the attack going to their lock. DamageFeedbackPacket then plays hit feedback on the host-picked enemy, so the client can see its projectile hit enemy A while feedback/death lands on enemy B.

(4) Nothing covers it: grep for "Lock Target"/"preferredTarget" in mod source has zero matches outside this audit; PlayerNetworkData.Shared (Components\PlayerNetworkData.cs:13-21) carries only Move/Sprint/Interact/CallNight/CommandUnits; no Sync class touches ManualAttack. HpSync only corrects HP values after the fact — it cannot correct WHICH enemy was hit.

Severity: high, not critical — HP state stays consistent via host authority + HpSync, and it does not directly decide win/lose; but a vanilla combat feature silently misdirects the active ability for clients, and the host's lock press hijacks the authoritative targeting of other players' pawns. That is visibly wrong combat.

(2) Death-spawn desync mechanics confirmed: Hp.TakeDamage IL death branch does `ldfld GameObject Hp::enemyToSpawnOnDeath` -> Object::Instantiate(transform.position/rotation) -> `call Spawn::AdjustEnemyParametersAfterSpawn(GameObject, bool elite)` with no Identifier ever attached. HpPatch.TakeDamage (Main\Patches\HpPatch.cs:34-49) lets the server run the full original, and HpSync.Handle (Main\Network\Sync\HpSync.cs:106-132) replays deaths on clients with AllowHealthChangeOnClient=true, which also runs the full original body — so BOTH peers locally instantiate an un-identified copy. That copy is invisible to every sync (HpSync/PositionSync/EnemyPathfinderSync all iterate Identifier.GetIdentifiers(IdentifierType.Enemy)); on clients it never receives a path (PathFinderMovementEnemyPatch suppresses Original/BackupOnPathComplete on non-server, so it stays effectively frozen) and it survives the dawn cleanup, which kills only Identifier.GetGameObjects(IdentifierType.Enemy) (Main\Network\PacketHandler.cs:163-168). On the host it is a real EnemyOwned enemy that blocks the night-to-day check (DayNightCyclePatch.cs:34) until killed. One overstatement in the claim: the client ghost is not strictly immortal — HpPatch.TakeDamage falls through to original when identifier == null, so the client's own attacks can locally kill it (and locally spawn its coins); but host kills never propagate, so the copies live and die independently. Hp::enemyToSpawnOnDeath is a public serialized field (T:Hp IL), so natively-set prefabs are possible but unverifiable from IL (serialized data); today the practically certain symptom is the inert mutator, and the ghost desync becomes guaranteed the moment the mutator wiring is restored naively — which is why both halves must ship together.

Not already covered: no packet/sync touches enemyToSpawnOnDeath, and the one component that could mint identified ad-hoc enemies (Main\PrefabSpawner.cs, Spawn(ushort id, EnemyType)) is dead code — nothing in the mod instantiates or calls it.
