confirmedGaps.what: PlayerNetworkData.Shared.Slowed is written only on the host (PlayerMovementPatch.Update: `if (Plugin.Instance.Network.Server) playerNetworkData.SharedData.Slowed = slowedFor.Value > 0f;`) but is never serialized: SyncPlayerInputPacket.Send/Receive write 7 of the 9 Shared fields, omitting Slowed (and CallNightFill), even though Shared.Set() copies it and Shared.Compare() compares it. Additionally, even if serialized, the owning client could never receive it: InputSync.Filter skips sending a player's own data back to its owner and InputSync.Handle early-returns for the local player.
confirmedGaps.divergence: Player positions are owner-authoritative (PositionSync: clients send only their own pawn; the host lerps its copy toward the client position with weight 0.35-1.0 at 20Hz). The velocity multiplier `velocity *= SharedData.Slowed ? 0.33f : 1f` therefore only ever bites on the host's own pawn. Client players are effectively IMMUNE to the vanilla on-hit slow debuff (PlayerMovement.Slow(duration) -> slowedFor; vanilla Update multiplies velocity by 0.33 while slowed): the client's authoritative sim always runs at full speed and drags the host's slowed copy along. Conversely, when the HOST is slowed, clients sim the host pawn at full speed and see it rubber-band. Note the asymmetry: ally and enemy slows ARE synced (SyncAllyPathfinderPacket.Slowed / SyncEnemyPathfinderPacket.Slowed), proving player Slowed was meant to be synced too.
confirmedGaps.fix: Three coordinated changes, consistent with existing mod patterns: (1) Serialize Slowed in BOTH player packets: add writer.Write(Data.Slowed)/Data.Slowed=reader.ReadBoolean() to SyncPlayerInputPacket.Send/Receive AND the Shared block of SyncPlayersPacket.Send/Receive — this makes remote pawns (notably the slowed host pawn on client screens) sim at 0.33x and kills the rubber-banding. (2) Owner-local derivation instead of host echo (InputSync deliberately never sends a player its own data — keep that): in PlayerMovementPatch.Update change line 133-136 to `if (Plugin.Instance.Network.Server || playerNetworkData.IsLocal) playerNetworkData.SharedData.Slowed = slowedFor.Value > 0f;` — this restores the Blood Wand self-slow for client wielders because BloodWandActiveAttEffect.Effect calls PlayerMovement.instance.Slow locally and the mod already points PlayerMovement.instance at the local pawn (PlayerMovementPatch.cs:84-87). Do NOT rely on HandleDamageFeedback/OnReceiveDamage to populate slowedFor — no such UnityEvent wiring exists (Hp.Initialize IL); the only Slow callers are DamagePlayerWhenEnteringRange.ApplyDamage and BloodWandActiveAttEffect.Effect. (3) SEPARATE follow-UP gap surfaced by this audit (file it independently): DamagePlayerWhenEnteringRange.Start binds to TagManager.instance.Players[0] forever, so its enter-range damage+slow mechanic ignores all client pawns entirely. Fix host-authoritatively: patch DamagePlayerWhenEnteringRange.Update (On. hook) to evaluate every pawn in TagManager.instance.Players on the host, and route the slow to the owning client via a small SlowPlayerPacket{PlayerId, Duration} sent with Network.Send(packet, handleLocal:true) whose handler calls PlayerMovement.Slow on the identified pawn (damage path already host-gated by HpPatch). Also consider guarding BloodWandActiveAttEffect.Effect host-side so a client's wand hit processed on the host does not slow the host's own pawn via the PlayerMovement.instance singleton misdirect.
confirmedGaps.reasoning: CONFIRMED MECHANICS (all re-derived): (1) Shared.Slowed is serialized by NO packet — SyncPlayerInputPacket.Send/Receive (C:\Users\nalar\tfmp_src\Main\Network\Packets\Sync\SyncPlayerInputPacket.cs:18-40) writes 7 fields omitting Slowed/CallNightFill, and the claim missed that a second packet exists, SyncPlayersPacket (Packets\Sync\SyncPlayersPacket.cs:23-66), which DOES carry CallNightFill but still omits Slowed. (2) SharedData.Slowed is written only under `if (Plugin.Instance.Network.Server)` (Patches\PlayerMovementPatch.cs:133-136) yet is the sole input to `velocity *= Slowed ? 0.33f : 1f` (line 139) on every peer; vanilla equivalent confirmed in IL (PlayerMovement.Slow -> slowedFor = Mathf.Max; decrement + ldc.r4 0,33 multiplier in PlayerMovement.MoveScript, called from vanilla Update — claim cited 'vanilla Update', minor slip). (3) Even if serialized, the owner could never receive it: BaseTargetSync.Update skips peers where Filter==true (BaseTargetSync.cs:94-97,136-139), InputSync.Filter returns true for the owner (InputSync.cs:52-55), and InputSync.Handle early-returns for LocalId (lines 62-69). (4) Player pawns are owner-authoritative: PositionSync.Targets client branch sends only the local pawn, host Handle lerps toward it (PositionSync.cs:75-97,125-157). (5) Ally/enemy slows ARE synced (SyncAllyPathfinderPacket/SyncEnemyPathfinderPacket Slowed fields) — asymmetry confirmed. So the gap is real: the 0.33x slow multiplier can only ever bite the host's own pawn on the host's own screen. NOT COVERED ELSEWHERE: checked SyncPlayersPacket, HpSync (health only), no patch exists for DamagePlayerWhenEnteringRange or BloodWandActiveAttEffect (grep: zero hits). HOWEVER, TWO CLAIM ERRORS TEMPER SEVERITY: (a) The claim's assertion that 'no IL call site exists in any damage method, so Slow is UnityEvent-wired' is FALSE — tf_callers.exe found direct call sites: DamagePlayerWhenEnteringRange.ApplyDamage (2 sites, before Hp.TakeDamage, IL confirmed) and BloodWandActiveAttEffect.Effect (slows PlayerMovement.instance). Hp.Initialize IL shows no OnReceiveDamage->Slow wiring, so the fix sketch's reliance on HandleDamageFeedback populating client slowedFor is unfounded. (b) The headline 'clients are immune to the on-hit slow' is mostly caused by a DIFFERENT vanilla bug: DamagePlayerWhenEnteringRange.Start binds permanently to TagManager.instance.Players[0] (IL confirmed, mod does not patch it), which with deterministic pawn creation order (PlayerManager.SetPrefab) is the host pawn on every machine — those enemies never even target client pawns (slow OR damage). The divergences truly attributable to THIS sync gap are: (i) when the host pawn is slowed, clients sim it full-speed and see 20Hz rubber-band corrections — visible but self-correcting via PositionSync (medium per rubric); (ii) a client wielding the Blood Wand never experiences its self-slow drawback (Effect sets slowedFor on the client's local authoritative pawn, but consumption reads always-false SharedData.Slowed) — genuinely wrong gameplay that does not self-correct, but limited to one optional weapon; and if Effect runs host-side for a client attack it slows the WRONG pawn (host's, via PlayerMovement.instance singleton). Real and worth fixing, but 'high' overstates the common case; the rubric's 'self-corrects or rarely matters' fits most manifestations -> medium.

mediumLowGaps.what: End-of-match score: LocalGamestate.WaitThenTriggerEndOfMatchScreen reads ScoreManager.VictoryGoldBonus = PlayerInteraction.instance.TrueBalance * victoryGoldBonusMultiplyer. PlayerInteraction.instance is set by every player object's Awake ('stsfld PlayerInteraction PlayerInteraction::instance' — last instantiated player wins), and the mod only mirrors GlobalData.Balance into the LOCAL player's 'balance' field (TreasuryUIPatch.cs:69, BuildingInteractorPatch.cs:44) and never rebinds PlayerInteraction.instance (it rebinds PlayerMovement.instance only — PlayerManager.cs:161-168). TrueBalance additionally adds TagManager.coins.Count + CoinSpawner.AllCoinsLeftToBeSpawned, which are per-peer local coin objects. This is the mod's own unresolved TODO (GlobalData.cs:16-19).
mediumLowGaps.divergence: On any peer where the last-instantiated player object is a remote puppet (typically the host, since the joining client's player is created last), VictoryGoldBonus is computed from balance=0, so the victory screen shows a visibly lower score than the other peer and a lower score is persisted/compared as the level high score. Even when instance happens to be the local player, the in-flight-coin terms differ per peer, so final scores never quite match.
mediumLowGaps.fix: Rebind PlayerInteraction.instance to the local player alongside PlayerMovement.instance in PlayerManager.InstantiatePlayer (PlayerManager.cs:161) and in PlayerMovementPatch.Update's IsLocal rebind; the existing TreasuryUIPatch mirror then keeps instance.balance == GlobalData.Balance. Optionally hook On.LocalGamestate.SetState to force-mirror once before the end screen so the bonus is computed from the synced shared balance on all peers.

mediumLowGaps.what: Healing Gold perk: vanilla Coin.Update, on every coin pickup, calls PlayerMovement.instance.Hp.Heal(healingGoldHealAmount * PlayerHealthRegenerationMultiplyer) — it heals the singleton player, not the coin's target. The mod binds PlayerMovement.instance to the LOCAL player on each peer (PlayerMovementPatch.cs:84-87, PlayerManager.cs:161-168), and HpPatch.Heal suppresses heals on clients (HpPatch.cs:67-80). Coins are simulated per-peer and only the host's pickups are authoritative, so on the host EVERY coin collected by ANY player (including the client's puppet) heals the host's own hero; the client-side heal of the client's hero is suppressed and never happens on the host.
mediumLowGaps.divergence: With Healing Gold equipped, the host's hero receives all coin-pickup healing for the whole team (2x intended healing) while the client's hero receives none — HpSync then makes this wrong attribution authoritative on both screens. The perk is effectively broken for clients and doubled for the host.
mediumLowGaps.fix: Patch On.Coin.Update: read the private 'target' field (Traverse, field verified in IL as 'PlayerInteraction Coin::target'); if target != null, temporarily swap PlayerMovement.instance to target.GetComponent<PlayerMovement>() around the original call and restore afterwards (same singleton-juggling pattern as EnemySpawnerPatch). Server applies the heal to the correct player's Hp and HpSync propagates it; client-side heals stay suppressed by HpPatch.

mediumLowGaps.what: The mod's static economy bookkeeping is never reset at level start. (a) GlobalData.Internal.NetWorth is only ever incremented (PlayerInteractionPatch.cs:104) and no code anywhere assigns it on match start — grep shows the only writes are AddCoin += and ResourceSync receive — so on the host it accumulates across restarts and across different levels for the whole session. (b) It also never receives starting gold, Treasure Hunter bonuses, or /add_coins cheats, because those routes set GlobalData.Balance only (EnemySpawnerPatch.cs:33, EnemySpawnerPatch.cs:52, CheatHandler.cs:56), whereas vanilla routes them through PlayerInteraction.AddCoin which increments networth (IL EnemySpawner.Start: 'callvirt System.Void PlayerInteraction::AddCoin' with goldBalanceAtStart; same in OnStartOfTheDay for the treasure bonus). (c) GlobalData.LocalBalanceDelta on clients is only zeroed by ConfirmBuild/CancelBuild for the local player (PacketHandler.cs:381,390) — a client that ends a match mid-fill (e.g. victory/defeat fires while coins are streamed into a CostDisplay via SpendCoins) carries a negative delta into the next level.
mediumLowGaps.divergence: The end-screen day-to-day networth graph (DayNightCyclePatch.cs:80-83 feeds levelData.dayToDayNetworth from GlobalData.NetWorth) is inflated by every previous match in the session and understated by start/treasure gold — on all peers, every match after the first. The stale client delta means a client can start the next match seeing (and being limited by) less gold than the host actually has, until their next confirmed or cancelled build resets the delta. Additionally the graph value adds TagManager.freeCoins.Count, a per-peer local coin-object count, so host and client record slightly different graphs even within one match.
mediumLowGaps.fix: In EnemySpawnerPatch.Start: on server set GlobalData.Internal.NetWorth = balance (mirrors vanilla AddCoin of start gold) instead of relying on increments; in the Treasure Hunter branch and CheatHandler also do Internal.NetWorth += bonus. On every peer reset GlobalData.LocalBalanceDelta = 0 in the same Start patch (or in SceneTransitionManagerPatch on level load). ResourceSync already propagates the corrected values; no new packets needed.

mediumLowGaps.what: HpPatch.TakeDamage (host) sends DamageFeedbackPacket BEFORE calling original and unconditionally — even when vanilla TakeDamage early-returns (target invulnerable, hp<=0, amount<=0 heal-redirect) or was called with invokeFeedbackEvents=false (e.g. Hp.TakeDamageSelfDrained passes false, HpSync corrections pass false on host loopback-free path). Vanilla only fires OnReceiveDamage when damage actually applied AND invokeFeedbackEvents is true.
mediumLowGaps.divergence: Clients invoke hp.OnReceiveDamage for hits that never landed on the host. Concrete consequence: ShieldOnFirstDamaged.Start subscribes OnDamaged to OnReceiveDamage; a spurious feedback consumes the unit's once-per-day shield on the client — OnDamaged sets Hp.invulnerable=true locally while its SetHpToMaxHp half is gated (HpPatch) — leaving the client unit 'shielded' with stale HP and (per the invulnerable gap) dropping subsequent host HP corrections; meanwhile the host's shield is still available, so shield state permanently diverges for that day. Other listeners (hit flashes, PlayerCharacterAudio.OnDmg) misfire cosmetically.
mediumLowGaps.fix: In HpPatch.TakeDamage capture preHp = self.HpValue and the early-return conditions; call original first, then send DamageFeedbackPacket only if (invokeFeedbackEvents && amount > 0 && !self.invulnerable-at-entry && preHp > 0) — i.e. mirror vanilla's OnReceiveDamage condition.

mediumLowGaps.what: AutoRevive.Update (player auto-revive: dawn revive, timed night revive, Ring of Resurrection quick revive, Explosive Revival weapon) runs on every peer and calls Hp.Revive / Hp.ReviveAllUnitsWithTag directly — neither method is hooked by HpPatch, so clients execute them locally. The quickReviveAvailable flag is consumed per peer at locally-measured times (hasBeenKnockedOutFor += Time.deltaTime per peer).
mediumLowGaps.divergence: A client whose KO timer fires before the host's revives its player locally (possibly at a different health: minHealthOnRevive vs host's), and with Ring of Resurrection also mass-revives every Tag-7 unit locally via ReviveAllUnitsWithTag(100%) — HpSync then re-knocks each one out over the next seconds (visible mass revive/re-death flicker). Timing skew can also desync the once-per-night quickReviveAvailable flag between peers, changing which revive branch each peer takes for the rest of the night.
mediumLowGaps.fix: Hook On.AutoRevive.Update and run original only on the server (same pattern as UnitRespawnerForBuildingsPatch.RespawnAKnockedOutUnit); clients receive the revive (and the explosive-revival damage results) via HpSync/DamageFeedback. Keep the local revive-panel UI driven by the synced KnockedOut state.

mediumLowGaps.what: DamagePlayerWhenEnteringRange (level hazard that damages + slows a player entering its radius) caches TagManager.Players[0] in Start (playerTaggedObj/transformPlayer/playerHp/playerMovement) — a singleplayer assumption never multiplayer-ized. Its Update range-check and ApplyDamage (TakeDamage + PlayerMovement.Slow(slowPlayerFor)) therefore only ever consider one player object per peer.
mediumLowGaps.divergence: All players except Players[0] are completely immune to these hazards (the host component never checks them, and client-side TakeDamage on identified players is gated anyway). If peer-local TagManager registration order differs, each peer even watches a DIFFERENT player, so the slow (PlayerMovement.slowedFor, unhooked) fires on different players on different machines, fighting PositionSync for the slow duration.
mediumLowGaps.fix: Patch On.DamagePlayerWhenEnteringRange.Update: on the host iterate Plugin.Instance.PlayerManager.GetAllPlayerData() player objects, run the range/velocity check and TakeDamage per player; replicate the slow to the owning client with a small packet (or piggyback slowedFor on SyncPlayerInput/PositionSync) so the locally-simulated player actually moves slow.

mediumLowGaps.what: The mod's host-side gather (CommandUnitsPatch.HandleNotCommanding) uses only TagManager.MeasureDistanceToTaggedObject, dropping vanilla Update's special case for Flying-tagged units (2D xz distance vs attractRange); and the reimplemented PlaceCommandedUnitsAndCalculateTargetPositions always uses the ground 'nearestConstraint' for AstarPath.GetNearest, dropping vanilla's 'unit.Flying ? nearestConstraintAir : nearestConstraint' selection.
mediumLowGaps.divergence: Flying units (elevated transforms) often fail the 3D distance check, so they are hard or impossible to pick up when commanding; when they are placed, their HomePosition is snapped to the ground graph instead of the air graph, giving wrong/unreachable home positions. The host computes and syncs these wrong homes (CommandPlacePacket/AllyPathfinderSync), so all peers consistently get the broken behavior.
mediumLowGaps.fix: Host-side only (no protocol change): in HandleNotCommanding add the Flying-tag branch using Vector2(xz) distance; in the placement patch read Traverse.Create(self).Field<NNConstraint>("nearestConstraintAir") (verified field) and select it per unit.Flying before GetNearest, mirroring current vanilla IL. Synced HomePositions then become correct for everyone.

mediumLowGaps.what: PathfindMovementPlayerunit.holdPosition is set on all peers via CommandHoldPositionPacket, but it is not in SyncAllyPathfinderPacket, and the only autonomous clear path — OnDawn_BeforeSunrise's formation reset (homePosition=homePositionOriginal; set_HoldPosition(false)) — is patched to run on the SERVER only with no packet mirroring the clear.
mediumLowGaps.divergence: When the host has ResetUnitFormationEveryMorning enabled, every dawn the host clears hold on all units while clients keep holdPosition=true indefinitely: hold-position markers stay visible on client screens for units that are actually walking home (HomePosition itself self-corrects via AllyPathfinderSync), and with the Commander perk the client's AutoAttack.DamageMultiplyer keeps the hold buff applied (set_HoldPosition side effect) while the host's was reverted — a persistent, never-corrected flag/stat divergence (damage outcomes stay correct only because client damage is gated by HpPatch).
mediumLowGaps.fix: Add a HoldPosition bool to SyncAllyPathfinderPacket (AllyPathfinderSync already targets every PathfindMovementPlayerunit): host writes pathfinder.HoldPosition into the packet; Handle applies it via the property setter so the commander-buff side effects replay symmetrically (Hp scaling stays gated by HpPatch and corrected by HpSync). This also future-proofs any other host-only clear path.

mediumLowGaps.what: UnitDashJump (dash-jump ally units) is completely unpatched — no mod reference exists. Its Update runs on host AND clients with local cooldown timers and local target acquisition (AutoAttack.FindAutoAttackTarget from the unit's local position), and DashJumpAnimation locally animates the transform through the air, toggles Hp.invulnerable true/false, disables/enables the movement component, fires weaponOnLanding, and calls set_HoldPosition.
mediumLowGaps.divergence: Clients trigger jumps at different times and toward different (locally-acquired, latency-shifted) targets than the host: the unit visibly leaps to one enemy on the client while the host's authoritative unit jumped elsewhere or not at all, then PositionSync (Ally deviance, up to 8 units before snap) drags it back mid-air. Client-side Hp.invulnerable and HoldPosition windows also diverge (no damage impact since HpPatch gates client damage, but visuals/markers and local prediction churn).
mediumLowGaps.fix: Follow the PathfindMovementPlayerunitPatch pattern: hook On.UnitDashJump.Update and early-return on clients so only the host simulates jumps (PositionSync+PositionErrorSmoother already move the client's unit). For visual parity, optionally have the host broadcast a small DashJumpPacket (unit IdentifierData + target id) handled by starting the animation client-side, like DamageFeedbackPacket does for hit feedback.

mediumLowGaps.what: ScoreManager.currentScore accumulates independently on each peer (OnDawn_BeforeSunrise → CalculateEndOfNightScore, invoked from both peers' SwitchToDayCoroutine). Its inputs are only approximately synced: the time bonus reads EnemySpawner.LastSpawnPeriodDuration, which each peer integrates locally per-frame (EnemySpawnerPatch.Update line 63-64 adds Time.deltaTime on all peers) and which stops on the client only when the last EnemySpawnPacket arrives, i.e. later than on the host by network latency; the protection bonus counts BuildingInteractor.KnockedOutTonight, which depends on HpSync arrival timing around the dawn boundary; the no-retry bonuses read each peer's local MatchSave.hadRestarts. There is no packet carrying the host's score.
mediumLowGaps.divergence: Host and client end-of-night score popups and final victory/defeat totals differ by a few points per night (rounding of the InverseLerp time bonus) and can differ grossly if a building knockout or hadRestarts flag lands on one peer only. Each peer then persists its own different highscore/dayToDayScore/meta XP. Self-limiting but permanently visible (players comparing victory screens see different numbers).
mediumLowGaps.fix: Make the host's score authoritative: extend DayNightPacket(Day) (or add a small ScorePacket sent alongside it) with the host's ScoreManager.currentScore after CalculateEndOfNightScore; clients Traverse-set the private 'currentScore' field (name verified in IL) before their own end screens read it. Also include hadRestarts so no-retry bonuses agree.

mediumLowGaps.what: Vanilla PlayerMovement.Update supports an alternative movement input: `if (input.GetButton("Move Towards Mouse"))` it computes the move vector from Input.mousePosition relative to screen center. The mod's full replacement of PlayerMovement.Update only consumes SharedData.MoveHorizontal/MoveVertical, and PlayerNetworkData.Update only samples the "Move Horizontal"/"Move Vertical" axes — "Move Towards Mouse" is read by vanilla but never captured into the synced SharedData (string appears nowhere in mod source).
mediumLowGaps.divergence: Not peer-divergent (the pawn stays still on all peers), but a whole input channel is dead in multiplayer: any player using the mouse-movement control scheme cannot move their character at all. This matches the audit question 'is any input axis/button consumed by vanilla code but never in the synced SharedData?' — this and Lock Target are the two such buttons ("Change Timescale" is also dropped, but that is the correct call in MP).
mediumLowGaps.fix: No protocol change needed: in PlayerNetworkData.Update, replicate vanilla's mouse math — when `input.GetButton("Move Towards Mouse")`, compute the normalized direction from screen center to Input.mousePosition and write it into SharedData.MoveHorizontal/MoveVertical (clamped to magnitude 1). It then flows through the existing InputSync/SyncPlayerInputPacket path unchanged.

mediumLowGaps.what: DayNightCyclePatch.Update fully replaces vanilla DayNightCycle.Update without calling original, keeping only the host's switch-to-day check. Vanilla Update also implements automatedDaytime (counts down remainingAutoDayTime during day; on expiry: ChoiceManager.CancelChoice() + UIFrameManager.CloseAllFrames() + SwitchToNight()) and automatedNighttime (auto switch-to-day timer). Neither timer runs on ANY peer now.
mediumLowGaps.divergence: On levels/scripts using automated day or night timers (DayNightCycle.automatedDaytime/automatedNighttime, e.g. AutoDayNight-driven sequences), night never auto-falls and day never auto-breaks — the match soft-locks on the day phase unless a manual night call exists. The suppressed path is also vanilla's only flow that force-closes an open build-choice UI at nightfall, so a player can sit in ChoiceManager while the world advances around them (the mod only cancels a choice for the same building or negative balance — BuildSlotPatch.HandleUpgrade, BuildingInteractorPatch.Update).
mediumLowGaps.fix: In DayNightCyclePatch.Update, after the server-only night check, replicate the automated-timer branches on the host only (count remainingAutoDayTime/remainingAutoNightTime via Traverse — both fields verified in IL) and emit the existing DayNightPacket (Night via NightCallPatch.TriggerNightFall path, Day via the current packet), so timed levels advance and the CancelChoice/CloseAllFrames flow fires through the already-synced transitions.

mediumLowGaps.what: Shrine progress (Shrine.currentXp via MakeProgressAndUpdateBar) is fed by Hp.TakeDamage's death hook Shrine.DeathOfUnitAt(position, HpOriginal), which range-checks the death position against each shrine's collectionRange. This executes independently on each peer: on the host at the true death position, on the client at the HpSync-lagged position/time (HpSync.Handle replays TakeDamage with AllowHealthChangeOnClient=true). Deaths near the collectionRange edge count on one peer and not the other, and the mass-kill on day-switch (PacketHandler.HandleDayNight TakeDamage 9999) credits client shrines at wherever the lagged enemies stood.
mediumLowGaps.divergence: Shrine XP bars drift between peers; a shrine can be activated on the host but not yet (or never) on a client. ActivateShrine toggles enableOnComplete/disableOnComplete objects and its ParentBuildSlot state, so a client can see a locked/unlocked shrine building inconsistent with the host; income is host-authoritative (TemplePrayers.EnableEffectAtDusk reads host state) so gold stays correct, but the client's world state and build options around the shrine are wrong.
mediumLowGaps.fix: Make shrine progress host-authoritative: gate Shrine.DeathOfUnitAt (or MakeProgressAndUpdateBar) behind Plugin.Instance.Network.Server, and add a small ShrineSync (BaseTargetSync keyed by the shrine's ParentBuildSlot Identifier) carrying currentXp + shrineHasBeenActivated; on clients write currentXp via Traverse and call ActivateShrine(false) when the flag flips.

mediumLowGaps.what: HpSync.Handle applies upward corrections as hp.Heal(difference), but Hp.Heal multiplies the amount by the private healingMultiplyer field (hp += amount * healingMultiplyer), which Hp.Start sets to PerkManager.healingSpirits_healMulti for player-tagged units when the Healing Spirits perk is equipped. Heal also early-returns when local hp<=0.
mediumLowGaps.divergence: With Healing Spirits equipped (now synced to both peers), every upward HpSync correction on affected units overshoots by the multiplier, the next packet corrects back down via exact TakeDamage, producing client-side HP oscillation around the host value while a unit is being healed; convergence is no longer one-packet. Bounded by sync, no kill/KO impact.
mediumLowGaps.fix: In HpSync.Handle, write the value exactly: Traverse.Create(hp).Field<float>("healingMultiplyer") — temporarily set 1 around Heal — or set the private "hp" field (System.Single, verified) directly to sync.Hp and invoke OnHpChange, keeping Revive/TakeDamage for the KO transitions.

mediumLowGaps.what: Auto/manual attacks still simulate fully on clients; HpPatch blocks only the Hp mutation. Weapon.DealDamage gates its post-damage effects on the CALCULATED damage (V_4 != 0), not on whether TakeDamage applied, so clients run additionalWeaponEffects and the slowsFastEnemiesFor branch (PathfindMovement::Slow) against whatever target their locally-randomized auto-attack picked (TargetPriority.FindRandomObjectInRange / AutoAttackLowestInRangeMulti.Update use Random.value).
mediumLowGaps.divergence: PathfindMovementEnemy.slowedFor/speedWhenSlowed diverge per peer: a client slows enemies its local sim hit while the host slowed different ones, so client-predicted enemy movement is wrong for the slow duration and PositionSync/EnemyPathfinderSync must rubber-band it. Heal-type effects stay safe (HealOnKill gets targetKilled=false on clients; HealthSteal heals via gated Hp.Heal). Net effect is movement jitter, not outcome changes.
mediumLowGaps.fix: Cheapest consistent option: in a Weapon.DealDamage hook (or PathfindMovement.Slow hook), skip Slow/additionalWeaponEffects on clients for identified targets (mirror the HpPatch gate); host-applied slows already reach clients indirectly through EnemyPathfinderSync/PositionSync corrections. Optionally add slowedFor to SyncEnemyPathfinderPacket for smoother prediction.

mediumLowGaps.what: With the experience-gain perk active, host-only RespawnAKnockedOutUnit (UnitRespawnerForBuildingsPatch gates it to server) multiplies currentExperienceMulti (clamped 1..3) and applies it to BOTH Hp (ScaleHp — synced via HpSync.maxHp) and every AutoAttack.DamageMultiplyer — which is never synced by any packet or Sync class.
mediumLowGaps.divergence: Client-side copies of veteran units keep base DamageMultiplyer forever while the host's is up to 3x. Real damage is host-authoritative (HpPatch blocks client TakeDamage on identified targets), so outcomes stay correct; the divergence is in client-local simulation/feedback that reads DamageMultiplyer (and any future client-side prediction of unit damage). Mostly invisible today — but it is a genuine unsynced host mutation of unit combat state.
mediumLowGaps.fix: Either include an optional DamageMultiplyer float in SyncHpPacket (it already targets every ally Hp and detects change cheaply), or send a tiny UnitXpPacket from the host respawn patch (unit IdentifierData + multiplier) applied to all AutoAttacks client-side. Low priority while client damage stays fully gated.

mediumLowGaps.what: DayNightCyclePatch.DuskCall iterates daytimeSensitiveObjects calling OnDusk(), THEN calls original(self) — but the current game's DayNightCycle.DuskCall itself iterates OnDuskEarly() AND OnDusk() over the same list. So every registered IDaytimeSensitive gets OnDusk twice per nightfall on every peer (EnemySpawner.OnDusk→OnStartOfTheNight is saved only by the spawningInProgress guard in StartSpawning; other handlers like TreasuryUI and any mod-registered objects run twice unguarded).
mediumLowGaps.divergence: Identical on host and client (so no peer desync), but doubled dusk callbacks deviate from vanilla once any OnDusk handler is non-idempotent (e.g. ET's LocalMatchSaveLoad.OnDusk saves the ET run twice; future game updates adding dusk-triggered income/effects would double-fire). Mainly a correctness landmine in the match-flow path the mod owns.
mediumLowGaps.fix: Drop the manual OnDusk loop in DayNightCyclePatch.DuskCall (keep only the null-cleanup RemoveAt pass if that was the NRE-fix intent) and let original(self) do the single OnDuskEarly+OnDusk pass.

mediumLowGaps.what: Vanilla sprint gate is `sprinting = (Sprint || sprintingToggledOn) && (hp.HpPercentage >= 1f || canAlwaysSprintEquipped)` where PlayerMovement.Start sets canAlwaysSprintEquipped = PerkManager.IsEquipped(canAlwaysSprint). The mod's replacement Update hard-codes `sprinting.Value = (SprintButton || sprintingToggledOn) && hp.HpPercentage >= 1f`, dropping the canAlwaysSprintEquipped flag (never referenced anywhere in mod source).
mediumLowGaps.divergence: Symmetric across peers (every peer runs the same patched code), so no desync — but the 'can always sprint' perk is silently inert in multiplayer: a damaged player who equipped it cannot sprint, unlike vanilla. Reported because it is vanilla logic suppressed by a patch without replacement, the same failure class the audit targets, just with consistent (rather than divergent) wrongness.
mediumLowGaps.fix: In PlayerMovementPatch.Update, read `Traverse.Create(self).Field<bool>("canAlwaysSprintEquipped")` (field verified in IL) alongside heavyArmorEquipped/racingHorseEquipped and change line 137 to `... && (hp.HpPercentage >= 1f || canAlwaysSprintEquipped)`. Deterministic on all peers since the shared perk loadout is already synced via SyncLevelDataPacket.

mediumLowGaps.what: Shared.CallNightFill is sampled every frame from NightCall.currentFill (PlayerNetworkData.Update via Traverse) and is part of Shared.Set/Compare, but SyncPlayerInputPacket does not serialize it (only the dead, never-sent legacy SyncPlayersPacket does). Remote peers therefore always see 0 for other players' night-call charge.
mediumLowGaps.divergence: Cosmetic: NightCallPatch.HandleNightCallAudio scales the night-call charge audio from each player's SharedData.CallNightFill, so peers never hear other players charging the night horn (the start one-shot from CallNightButton still plays). Side effect: because Compare() includes the unserialized field, the local player's input packet fails comparison every frame while charging night, forcing a ~per-frame resend of SyncPlayerInput for no receiver-visible change (bandwidth waste only).
mediumLowGaps.fix: Add `writer.Write(Data.CallNightFill)` / `Data.CallNightFill = reader.ReadFloat()` to SyncPlayerInputPacket (alongside the Slowed fix; bump mod protocol/version). Alternatively, if the audio is not worth the bytes, remove CallNightFill from Shared.Compare so it stops forcing per-frame resends, and drop it from Set. Delete or repurpose the dead SyncPlayersPacket while there.

mediumLowGaps.what: Some enemy behaviour components roll UnityEngine.Random and mutate transforms locally on every peer because only the pathfinder is suppressed on clients: Moleman.Update (Random.value gate) + Moleman/<TeleportAnimation>d__24 (3× Random.Range, Moleman.InstantiateHere, direct transform repositioning) run unpatched on clients, as do KeyframedBoss.Update's Random.Range animation/stage rolls.
mediumLowGaps.divergence: Client Molemen dig/teleport to self-chosen random positions and are then snapped back by PositionSync — visible rubber-banding and momentary wrong positions (a Moleman can appear inside the base on one peer for a beat); purely visual since damage and authoritative position are host-side and continuously corrected.
mediumLowGaps.fix: Gate Moleman.Update's teleport branch (and similar self-moving enemy behaviours) behind Plugin.Instance.Network.Server with an On.Moleman.Update hook, letting PositionSync/PositionErrorSmoother move the client copy — same pattern as PathFinderMovementEnemyPatch.

rejectedClaims.why: Every factual element of the claim verifies against the IL and mod source: CommandUnitsPatch.cs:17/22-61 fully replaces CommandUnits.Update without calling orig; current vanilla Update IL reads 'Command Units Toggle' (GetButtonDown -> commandUnitsToggledOn), 'Smart Command Units' (GetButton, threaded as _smartCommand into OnUnitAdd/placement/CustomUpdate), and four 'Select All Army/Melee/Ranged/Heroes' branches calling TryToSelectUnits, ending with UnitProductionSelector::CustomUpdate; PlayerNetworkData.Shared carries only CommandUnitsButton and its Update reads only input.GetButton("Command Units"); tf_callers confirms OnUnitAdd <- {Update, TryToSelectUnits, UnitProductionSelector.CommandAllMyUnits}, CustomUpdate <- Update only, CommandAllMyUnits <- CustomUpdate only — all unreachable once Update is replaced; the patch hardcodes PlaceCommandedUnitsAndCalculateTargetPositions(false) at line 120 and its reimplementation lacks the vanilla _smartCommand-gated toBePlaced name-filter subset (verified in vanilla IL); vanilla OnUnitAdd calls UnitTypeDisplay::SetSelected which mod AddUnit omits. HOWEVER, this is not a sync/divergence gap: CommandUnitsPatch.Apply() is invoked unconditionally at plugin start (Plugin.cs:72), so the identical replacement runs on EVERY peer. The suppressed vanilla paths execute on zero machines, the dead state (commandUnitsToggledOn, UnitProductionSelector.currentSelectProgress, smart selections) never mutates anywhere, and host and client remain perfectly consistent with each other. No peer ever sees different gameplay than another peer — there is nothing to desync and nothing for a sync packet to correct. It is a symmetric feature regression versus the current game version (an entire tier of unit commanding is uniformly dead, even effectively in singleplayer while the mod is loaded), i.e. a feature-parity bug from a stale full-replacement patch, not unsynced divergent state. Under the audit's definition (state that mutates during play but is not synchronized so peers silently diverge) and this verifier's criterion (truly unsynced AND truly divergent), real=false. It absolutely deserves a fix ticket, but in a 'missing vanilla features / stale patches' bucket, not the sync-coverage report; the moment the features are reimplemented, the claimant's input-sync additions become mandatory or they WOULD become a real divergence source (e.g. host-only smart placement filtering).
